title: Diabetic Retinopathy Grading from Fundus Imaging (PWDR)
sub_domain: Retinal vasculature reconstruction with diabetic retinopathy ETDRS-grade categorical readout
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-049 fundus (analytical core) with a deterministic threshold function on retinal vasculature tortuosity, microaneurysm density, exudate area, and neovascularization to yield ETDRS diabetic-retinopathy severity grades {none, mild_NPDR, moderate_NPDR, severe_NPDR, PDR}. Universally-accepted clinical screening test — FDA-cleared autonomous AI screening (IDx-DR, EyeArt) plus ophthalmologist-graded surveillance. UTSW Department of Ophthalmology runs DR screening clinics; aligns with standard-of-care.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on {none, mild_NPDR, moderate_NPDR, severe_NPDR, PDR} ETDRS-grade ordinal readout

discrete_readout.threshold_function: ETDRS-grade(retina) = max_grade k such that lesion_count_k(retina) >= tau_k where lesion_count_k counts microaneurysms, hemorrhages, hard exudates, cotton-wool spots, IRMA, venous beading, neovascularization extracted from recovered vasculature reconstruction; tau_thresholds set per international ETDRS / ICDR criteria (Wilkinson 2003) — none: <1 MA; mild NPDR: 1-19 MA; moderate NPDR: 20+ MA OR specific lesions; severe NPDR: 4-2-1 rule; PDR: any neovascularization or vitreous hemorrhage
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: retinal vasculature reconstruction (vessel skeleton, lumen diameter, tortuosity, microaneurysm density map, hemorrhage/exudate segmentation) recovered from fundus image via L1-049 fundus analytical core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: ETDRS grading thresholds are step functions on lesion-count features (microaneurysm count, hemorrhage area, etc.) which are themselves Lipschitz-continuous functionals of the underlying vasculature reconstruction. The 4-2-1 rule decision boundary forms a piecewise-linear hypersurface in lesion-count space; smoothed-grading (interpolated severity) is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per unit lesion-count uncertainty. Inherits underlying-state stability from L1-049.

E.description: Diabetic Retinopathy Grading from Fundus Imaging (PWDR): wraps L1-049 fundus analytical core with the international ETDRS / ICDR clinical-grading rule. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-049): recover retinal vasculature reconstruction from color fundus photograph including vessel skeleton, lumen diameters, optical-disc and macular landmarks, plus segmented lesion masks (microaneurysms, hemorrhages, hard exudates, cotton-wool spots, IRMA, venous beading, neovascularization). Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply ETDRS-grade thresholds to lesion counts and topographic locations to yield categorical severity {none, mild_NPDR, moderate_NPDR, severe_NPDR, PDR}. The threshold function is piecewise-constant on lesion-count features and locally Lipschitz-continuous on the underlying vasculature reconstruction. Difficulty tier delta = 3 inherited from L1-049 plus a small ETDRS-rule complexity. Mismatch parameters: image_quality, pupil_dilation_state, media_opacity, peripheral_field_truncation, manual_vs_automated_lesion_segmentation_disagreement, grader_inter_rater_variability_calibration.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-049): I_fundus(x, y, RGB) -> {vessel_skeleton, microaneurysm_map, hemorrhage_mask, exudate_mask, neovascularization_mask, optic_disc_loc, macula_loc}. Stage 2 (ETDRS rule): label = ETDRS_threshold(lesion_counts, topographic_locations) per Wilkinson et al. 2003 ICDR criteria.
E.world_state_x: Per-retina ETDRS severity grade in {none, mild_NPDR, moderate_NPDR, severe_NPDR, PDR}; optionally per-quadrant grading for surveillance.
E.observation_y: Color fundus photograph (typically 45-degree field, 2000x2000 to 5000x5000 RGB); optionally widefield or ultrawidefield imaging.

G.dag: L.fundus_acquisition -> L.vessel_segmentation -> L.lesion_segmentation -> L.lesion_count_aggregation -> L.etdrs_threshold_classifier -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: vasculature_to_lesion: vessel-network reconstruction and lesion-mask reconstruction share the underlying retinal-photometric image; a coupled segmentation enforces topological consistency (lesions on or near vessels vs background; microaneurysms only on capillary terminals)

W.regime: Existence inherited from L1-049 fundus core. Uniqueness conditional on adequate image quality (no severe media opacity; pupil dilation enables peripheral retinal coverage); ETDRS-grade transition boundaries (the 4-2-1 rule) form measure-zero hypersurfaces in lesion-count space. Stability inherits L1-049's kappa_eff plus a small additive contribution from grader_inter_rater_variability_calibration. The threshold-continuity proof in discrete_readout demonstrates that ETDRS-grade is a well-defined function of lesion counts; misclassification probability scales linearly with lesion-count error away from the 4-2-1 hypersurface boundary. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled vasculature-reconstruction + ETDRS-threshold forward established by Wilkinson 2003 (foundational ICDR paper), Abramoff 2018 (FDA-cleared autonomous AI), Gulshan 2016 (deep-learning grading benchmarks), Ting 2017 (Asian-population validation), Bhaskaranand 2019 (real-world performance), and Solomon 2017 (DR screening guidelines).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [vessel + lesion segmentation via L1-049] + categorical-readout [ETDRS rule] | end-to-end deep neural [DeepDR, IDx-DR, EyeArt] with explicit physics-informed lesion-segmentation regularization
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-049 segmentation bound for vasculature/lesion recovery. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(ETDRS_hat != ETDRS_true) <= C_thresh * |lesion_count_error| / d(counts, 4-2-1_boundary). Achieves q=2 convergence on segmentation stage; classification stage inherits with additive grader-calibration floor.
C.complexity: O(H * W * (N_vessel_classes + N_lesion_classes)) for stage 1; O(N_lesions) for stage 2 ETDRS-threshold; total stage-1-dominated

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify fundus images into DR severity {none, mild, moderate, severe, PDR}' (would be data_driven_statistical with end-to-end CNN). Option-3 recast: 'Recover retinal vasculature + lesion segmentation via fundus physics; apply deterministic ETDRS-rule thresholds on lesion counts and topographic locations'. Recast is feasible because retinal vasculature has a well-defined photometric forward model and ETDRS criteria are deterministic functions of recovered lesion counts.

joint_well_posedness_references: Wilkinson CP et al. (2003) Proposed international clinical diabetic retinopathy and diabetic macular edema disease severity scales. Ophthalmology 110(9):1677-1682.

Abramoff MD, Lavin PT, Birch M, Shah N, Folk JC (2018) Pivotal trial of an autonomous AI-based diagnostic system for detection of diabetic retinopathy in primary care offices (IDx-DR). NPJ Digital Medicine 1:39.
Gulshan V et al. (2016) Development and validation of a deep learning algorithm for detection of diabetic retinopathy in retinal fundus photographs. JAMA 316(22):2402-2410.

Ting DSW et al. (2017) Development and validation of a deep learning system for diabetic retinopathy and related eye diseases using retinal images from multiethnic populations. JAMA 318(22):2211-2223.
Bhaskaranand M et al. (2019) The value of automated diabetic retinopathy screening with the EyeArt system. Ophthalmol Retina 3(8):658-664.
Solomon SD et al. (2017) Diabetic retinopathy: a position statement by the ADA. Diabetes Care 40(3):412-418.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-049 fundus is the analytical core (recovers vasculature + lesion segmentation); L1-513 wraps with ETDRS-grade thresholds. Different solution_space (1D ETDRS severity grade vs 2D vasculature/lesion masks); adds L.lesion_count_aggregation and L.etdrs_threshold_classifier primitives. d_principle ~ 0.40 reflects shared upstream segmentation primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Diabetic retinopathy is the leading cause of preventable blindness in working-age adults. Universal screening recommended annually for all type-1 and type-2 diabetes patients (ADA, AAO guidelines). FDA-cleared autonomous AI: IDx-DR (April 2018), EyeArt (August 2020). At UTSW, the Department of Ophthalmology operates DR screening clinics and collaborates with primary-care diabetes management. PWDR formulation provides physics-grounded explainability: grades are derived from explicit lesion counts on the recovered vasculature, not from black-box CNN outputs.
